full_reduction_note: a comb-quality nu(t) needs a PHASE-CONNECTED timing solution over all 665 obs (per-obs H-test gives only ~mHz; the recovery/comb lives at ~uHz). That is ~6.5 GB of events + a multi-hour PINT/tempo2 timing analysis + glitch handling -- a real reduction project, not a sandbox fold.
verdict: REAL NICER Vela data reduced: 430739 photons barycentred with PINT (NO HEASoft), Vela pulsation DETECTED at F0=11.19275 Hz (H=18.4; period ~89.34 ms) on a 40000-photon subsample of obsid 0020180102. PIPELINE PROVEN ON REAL DATA. a comb-quality nu(t) needs a PHASE-CONNECTED timing solution over all 665 obs (per-obs H-test gives only ~mHz; the recovery/comb lives at ~uHz). That is ~6.5 GB of events + a multi-hour PINT/tempo2 timing analysis + glitch handling -- a real reduction project, not a sandbox fold. No claim.
